title: Chicken and Noodles
instructions: Melt butter in large pot over medium heat.
Add flour, whisk until combined, and brown.
Slowly add chicken stock, whisking constantly.
Add between 2-4 cups water (2 cups will yield little to no broth, 4 cups will be more brothy like soup).
Bring to a boil, whisking frequently to keep roux from clumping.
Add noodles.
Cook according to noodle packaging, stirring occasionally, adding spices in the last minute or two.
While noodles are cooking, shred white meat from rotisserie chicken.
Stir chicken into noodles after removing from heat
Serve in bowls.

title: Al Bundy's Chili

ingredientList.prep: ribs and seeds removed and diced
ribs and seeds removed and diced

instructions: Heat a drizzle of oil in large pot over medium-high heat.
Cook garlic, onions, green peppers, and jalapeños 2-3 minutes, until fragrant and onion is translucent.
Add ground beef, chili powder, paprika, cayenne pepper, oregano, cumin, salt and pepper.
Cook until beef is browned through.
Add beef stock and crushed tomatoes and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes.
While chili simmers, bring 1 cup water to a boil in a small pot.
Add rice and remove from heat.
Serve chili over rice.

title: Broccoli Cheddar Soup

ingredientList.prep: florets only or florets and stems

instructions: Dice onion, mince garlic, and peel and cut carrots into thin slices.
In a medium pan, add 1 Tbsp butter and diced onion. Sauté over medium heat until onion is translucent and barely browned, about 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook about 30 seconds, stirring constantly. Remove pan from heat and set aside.
In a large pot, add remaining butter and flour and cook over medium heat for 3-5 minutes, whisking constantly, until flour is thickened.
Slowly add chicken stock, whisking constantly.
Slowly add half & half, whisking constantly.
Allow mixture to simmer over low heat for 15-20 minutes, whisking intermittently to re-incorporate skin.
After simmering 15-20 minutes, add broccoli, carrots, and onion and garlic previously set aside.
Add salt, pepper, paprika, cayenne pepper and stir to combine.
Allow to simmer over low heat for 20-25 minutes, whisking intermittently to re-incorporate skin.

While soup simmers, grate the cheese. After simmering 20-25 minutes, add most of the cheese, reserving a small amount for garnishing if desired. Stir in cheese until melted and fully incorporated, about 1 minute.
Transfer to bowls and serve.

title: Corn Pudding
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°.
In a large bowl, melt butter.
Drain corn and mix with butter and sour cream.
Sprinkle in cornbread mix and mix well.
Transfer to large baking dish.
Bake for 1 hour.
Remove from oven when you can stick a knife into pudding and it comes out clean.

title: Corn and Zucchini Sauté
instructions: Drain corn. Halve zucchini lengthwise and slice into 1/4 inch pieces. Mince garlic.
In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add zucchini and garlic and cook 1-2 minutes until zucchini is bright green.
Add corn and season with salt and pepper.
Cook, tossing, until corn is heated through, 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in basil and white-wine vinegar and serve.

title: Lemony Chicken Spaghetti
instructions: Halve zucchini lengthwise and cut into 1/2 inch pieces. Quarter and zest lemon. Mince garlic. Slice chicken into strips or bite sized pieces.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
Add spaghetti and cook 9-11 minutes.
Reserve 1 cup pasta cooking water, then drain.
While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high, add zucchini and cook until zucchini is browned and softened, 4-6 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Transfer zucchini to a plate and wipe out pan.
Meanwhile, season chicken all over with Italian seasoning, salt and pepper.
Once zucchini is done, heat a drizzle of cooking oil in same pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until browned and cooked through.
Heat a drizzle of olive oil in pot used for spaghetti over medium-high heat. Add garlic, half the lemon zest and a pinch of chili flakes. Cook, stirring until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in 1/2 cup reserved pasta cooking water, stock concentrate, and juice from two lemon wedges. Simmer until thickened, 1-2 minutes. Turn off heat.
Add drained spaghetti, zucchini, sour cream, and butter to pot with sauce, toss to coat.
Add half the Parmesan and season with salt and pepper.
Divide pasta between bowls, top with chicken, remaining Parmesan, remaining lemon zest, and a pinch of chili flakes. Serve with remaining lemon wedges on the side.

title: Pan-Fried Pork Chops
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°.
Season pork chops with salt and pepper.
Melt butter in an oven-proof skillet over medium-high heat.
Add pork chops and a few sprigs of thyme and sear until chops are golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer pork chops in skillet to oven and cook through, about 12 minutes (pork is safe at 145°).
Serve immediately.

title: Chicken/Shrimp Quinoa Bowls
instructions: In a small pot, bring 1 cup water to a boil.
Add quinoa, reduce heat to low, and simmer until water is absorbed.
While water comes to a boil, cut chicken into chunks or thaw and de-tail shrimp.
In a large bowl, mix olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, thyme, black pepper and cayenne pepper.
Add chicken or shrimp to mixture and stir to coat.
In a medium pan, cook over medium heat until chicken is cooked through or shrimp is heated through.
While cooking, shred lettuce and place into bowls. Halve and slice avocado. Place avocado and broccoli florets over lettuce.
Once cooked, top bowls with quinoa and chicken or shrimp and serve with dressing of choice.

title: Chicken Ramen

ingredientList.prep: sliced, with whites and greens separated

instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
Peel and mince garlic and ginger. Thinly slice mushrooms. Thinly slice scallions and separate whites from greens.
Slice chicken into strips or bite-sized pieces.
In a small bowl, combine sesame seeds, half the garlic, half the sesame oil, a drizzle of cooking oil, chili flakes, and a big pinch of salt. Microwave for 30 seconds and set aside.
Once water is boiling, add ramen noodles and cook according to package. Drain, then toss noodles with a drizzle of cooking oil. Set aside.
Heat a drizzle of cooking oil in pot used for noodles over medium-high heat. Add scallion whites, ginger and remaining garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in 4 cups water, bouillon cubes, and soy sauce. Bring to a boil, then cover and reduce heat to low.
Meanwhile, heat cooking oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until browned and cooked through. Drizzle with remaining sesame oil and mix to coat.
Add mushrooms and a pinch of salt to pan and cook until browned and softened, about 4-5 minutes.
Divide ramen noodles between bowls. Pour broth over noodles and stir in desired amount of chili-garlic oil. Top with chicken, mushrooms, scallion greens and crispy onions and serve.

title: Balsamic Parmesan Risotto

ingredientList.prep: sliced, with whites and greens separated

instructions: Trim and thinly slice scallions, separating whites from greens. Halve tomatoes. Trim and dice zucchini into 1/2-inch pieces.
Heat a drizzle of cooking oil into a large pan over medium heat. Add scallion whites and cook until softened, about 1 minute.
Add rice and 2 tsp Tuscan Heat Spice. Stir until rice is translucent, 1–2 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup of stock, stirring until liquid has mostly absorbed. Repeat 1/2 cup at a time until rice is al dente and mixture is creamy, 25–30 minutes.
Once rice has cooked about 20 minutes, heat 1 tsp basil oil in a separate pan over medium-high heat. Add zucchini and 1 tsp Tuscan Heat Spice. Cook until tender and lightly browned, 4–6 minutes.
Add tomatoes and balsamic vinegar to pan with zucchini. Cook until tomatoes have softened, 2–3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper and turn off heat.
Once risotto is done, stir in butter and half the Parmesan. Drizzle with basil oil to taste and serve topped with remaining Parmesan and vegetables.

title: Pub-Style Shepherd's Pie
instructions: Heat broiler to high.
Dice potatoes into 1/2-inch pieces. Trim, peel and halve carrots lengthwise, then slice into 1/4 inch slices. Finely dice celery. Peel and finely chop onion.
Place potatoes in a medium pot with enough water to cover by 2 inches. Bring to a boil and cook until tender, 15-20 minutes. Drain and return potatoes to pot.
Mash potatoes with sour cream, butter and 1/2 tsp thyme until smooth, adding splashes of water as needed. Season with salt and pepper.
While potatoes cook, heat a drizzle of oil in a medium oven-proof pan over medium-high heat. Add carrot, season with salt and pepper and cook 2-3 minutes.
Add celery, onion, and a large drizzle of oil to pan with carrots, season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring, until veggies are tender, 5-7 minutes.
Stir in garlic powder and 1/2 tsp thyme and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add beef to pan with veggies and season with salt and pepper. Cook, breaking up meat into pieces, until browned and cooked through, about 4-6 minutes.
Add tomato paste and flour and cook, stirring, until thoroughly combined, 1 minute.
Gradually pour in bouillon-water mixture and bring to a boil. Cook until mixture is very thick, 1-2 minutes. Turn off heat.
Top beef filling with an even layer of mashed potatoes, leaving a gap around the edge of the pan. Sprinkle evenly with cheddar.
Broil until browned, 3-4 minutes. Serve directly from pan.

title: Tortellini Soup
instructions: Peel and slice carrots into 1/4 inch pieces. Dice onion. Mince garlic.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, salt and a few grinds of pepper. Cook, stirring, until vegetables begin to soften, about 8 minutes.
Add balsamic vinegar, garlic, tomatoes, stock, thyme and red pepper flakes. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring water in a separate pot to a boil. Cook the tortellini according to the package directions until al dente.
Once soup has simmered for 30 minutes, transfer to a blender and blend until it is a creamy consistency. Return soup to pot.
Add tortellini to soup and simmer for 2 more minutes, breaking up any shells that are stuck together.
Season to taste with more salt and pepper and serve.

title: Vegetable Beef Soup

ingredientList.prep: or chuck steak, cut into cubes

instructions: Peel and cut potatoes into 1/2-inch cubes. Peel and slice carrots into 1/4-inch slices. Finely chop onion and celery. Mince garlic.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over high heat.
Pat beef dry with paper towels and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Brown beef aggressively, then remove beef and set aside.
Add garlic and onion and cook for 2 minutes.
Add carrots and celery and cook 2 more minutes.
Stir in flour, then slowly pour in beef broth while constantly stirring.
Add red wine vinegar, water, tomato paste, bay leaves and thyme and stir well.
Add the beef back into the pot.
Cover, reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 1 hour 15 minutes.
Add potatoes and simmer for 20 more minutes uncovered. Add cooked mushrooms (optional) in the last 5 minutes.
Season to taste with more salt and pepper and serve.
Optional: Buttery mushrooms — Melt butter in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and cook for about 5 minutes until browned. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Spicy Garlic Shrimp
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add shrimp and cook 2–3 minutes per side until pink.
Stir in soy sauce, sesame oil, and red pepper flakes.
Top with sliced scallions and serve.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Quick Chicken Pesto Pasta
instructions: Cook pasta according to package directions.
Heat olive oil in a skillet and cook chicken until browned.
Drain pasta and add to skillet with chicken.
Stir in pesto and Parmesan cheese.
Season with salt and pepper and serve.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Italian Chopped Salad
instructions: Place chopped lettuce in a large bowl.
Add tomatoes, onion, and cucumber.
Toss with Italian dressing.
Top with shaved Parmesan and serve.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Spicy Sesame Noodle Salad
instructions: Cook ramen noodles and rinse with cold water.
Combine cabbage and carrot in a bowl.
Whisk sesame oil, soy sauce, and sriracha.
Toss noodles and vegetables with dressing.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Chicken Caesar Salad
instructions: Season and cook chicken in a skillet until fully cooked.
Place lettuce in a bowl.
Slice chicken and add to lettuce.
Add croutons and Parmesan cheese.
Toss with Caesar dressing and serve.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Garlic Parmesan Roasted Potatoes
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°.
Toss potatoes with olive oil, garlic, salt and pepper.
Spread on a baking sheet.
Roast until golden and crispy, about 25 minutes.
Sprinkle with Parmesan before serving.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Spicy Garlic Green Beans
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a skillet.
Add garlic and cook briefly.
Add green beans and sauté until tender-crisp.
Stir in soy sauce and red pepper flakes.
Serve warm.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Chocolate Mug Cake
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a microwave-safe mug.
Mix until smooth.
Microwave for about 1 minute.
Let cool slightly before eating.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Cinnamon Apple Crisp
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°.
Place apples in a baking dish.
Mix oats, sugar, cinnamon, and butter.
Spread mixture over apples.
Bake until golden and bubbly.
This is a temporary recipe

title: Vanilla Yogurt Berry Parfait
instructions: Spoon yogurt into a bowl or glass.
Add a layer of berries.
Top with granola.
Drizzle with honey.
Serve immediately.
This is a temporary recipe
